data.name: SABT_Message_ActivationWarning
data.value: The belt tensioner motors will be activated. Proceed?

data.name: SABT_Message_SimHubLicenseRequired
data.value: Your installation of SimHub is not licensed, so telemetry updates will be limited to 10Hz

data.name: SABT_Message_Setup_TurnOffPower
data.value: We'll now set up the motors. The first step is to TURN OFF THE POWER to the tensioner. Have you done this?

data.name: SABT_Message_Setup_Complete
data.value: Great, the RIGHT MOTOR has been configured. Both motors are now ready to go...

data.name: SABT_Message_Setup_PlugInLeftMotor
data.value: Now we need to PLUG IN THE LEFT MOTOR ONLY. Any of the ports on the controller will do. Once connected, plug in the power. Have you done this?

data.name: SABT_Message_Setup_PlugInRightMotor
data.value: Great, the LEFT MOTOR has been configured. Now PLUG IN THE RIGHT MOTOR, leaving the other motor still connected. Have you done this?

data.name: SABT_Message_Setup_AlreadySetUp
data.value: You appear to already be set up correctly (both motors are connected)!

data.name: SABT_Message_Setup_FailedToSetLeftMotor
data.value: Failed to set identifier for the LEFT MOTOR. Please ensure only one motor is plugged in and powered

data.name: SABT_Message_Setup_FailedToSetRightMotor
data.value: Failed to set identifier for the RIGHT MOTOR. Please ensure both motors are plugged in and powered

data.name: SABT_Message_NoDeviceDetected
data.value: No device detected. You must select a serial device before performing setup!

data.name: SABT_Message_DeviceFailure
data.value: The device could not be found, or motors did not respond. The 'Enable Motors' option has been automatically switched off

data.name: SABT_Label_Connection
data.value: • Select a serial port from the drop-down list below. Only ports that appear to be the belt tensioner device will be shown. If the list is empty, check that the controller is plugged in. It should appear even without power or any motors connected. • Future launches of SimHub should remember your port selection and reconnect automatically. However the 'Enable Motors' option will be reset every launch unless 'Start Automatically' is also enabled. • If this is your first time setting up, you'll need to then click 'Setup Motors' and follow the instructions given. You'll only need to do this once, as each motor stores its configuration once set.

data.name: SABT_Label_IdleTension
data.value: • Always applied while not driving (no effect while driving) • Behaves like the retraction spring on an inertia reel belt • Set to zero to disable; limited to 25% of maximum motor torque

data.name: SABT_Label_DrivingTension
data.value: • The minimum and maximum tension to be applied while driving • Increasing the minimum will apply a constant tension and enhance acceleration and jumping effects • Reducing the maximum will result in a reduction of overall tension strength

data.name: SABT_Label_LeftRightBias
data.value: • If tension feels unbalanced between sides, adjust this left or right to bias towards that side • Check and correct your belt path and contact points before resorting to using this setting

data.name: SABT_Label_Cornering
data.value: • How much cornering forces should affect the tension differential between the two belts

data.name: SABT_Label_Braking
data.value: • How much decceleration forces should increase the tension on both belts

data.name: SABT_Label_Acceleration
data.value: • How much acceleration forces should decrease the tension on both belts • Set minimum 'Driving Tension' to non-zero for best results (see 'Tension' tab)

data.name: SABT_Label_Jumping
data.value: • How much jumping should decrease the tension on both belts • Set minimum 'Driving Tension' to non-zero for best results (see 'Tension' tab)

data.name: SABT_Label_SetMinimumTensionToNonZero
data.value: • Set minimum 'Driving Tension' to non-zero for best results (see 'Tension' tab)

data.name: SABT_Label_Landing
data.value: • How much landing should increase the tension on both belts

data.name: SABT_Label_Shifting
data.value: • Adds a more pronounced release of tension on upshift than standard telemetry offers • Set minimum 'Driving Tension' to non-zero for best results (see 'Tension' tab)

data.name: SABT_Label_TelemetryLimits
data.value: • The permitted value ranges for incoming telemetry (anything more or less will be clipped) • Set appropriately for the types of vehicles you mostly drive (defaults are fine for most) • Narrower ranges result in more aggressive effects, wider ranges result in greater subtlety • Run a replay to see live telemetry plotted above and to make adjustment easier

data.name: SABT_Label_SmoothingFactor
data.value: • How much the tension output should be smoothed

data.name: SABT_Text_SimHub
data.value: SimHub plugin based upon 'SimHub Plugin Demo' by SHWotever:

data.name: SABT_Text_Repository
data.value: For more information, to report a bug or contribute, consult the project GitHub repository:

data.name: SABT_Text_Designer
data.value: Hardware and software design by George Wilkins:

data.name: SABT_Label_AutoCalibrate_Description
data.value: • Automatically adjusts the tensioner range to match the current car's peak telemetry • Ensures consistent belt tension feel across all vehicle classes (F1 to road cars) • When disabled, use the Surge/Sway/Heave limit sliders below to set ranges manually

data.name: SABT_Label_AdaptationSpeed_Description
data.value: • Faster: adapts quickly when switching vehicles (short memory, ~2 sec) • Slower: stable during a session, remembers peak forces longer (~2 min) • Default middle position works well for most cases

data.name: SABT_Label_AutoReconnectDelay_Description
data.value: • Seconds to wait before attempting to reconnect after a motor failure (e.g. back-driving slip) • The motors will automatically re-enable if reconnection succeeds • Set to a higher value if your power supply takes longer to recover from back-driving
